About: It is about the Blessed Virgin Mary.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Basilica of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It is associated with the Feast of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It is associated with the town of Šiluva.
It is associated with the Roman Catholic Church.
BelongsTo: It belongs to Roman Catholic tradition.
CreatedTo: It was created to be venerated.
HasA: It has a rock under the chapel altar.
It has a statue of Our Lady of Šiluva with Christ Child.
It has a mosaic wall of a blue background and golden mandorla.
It has two large mosaic panels on the side walls.
It has mosaics and stained glass by Albinas Elskus.
It has an altar frontal showing traditional Lithuanian wayside crosses.
It has a painting depicting the apparition.
It has a piece of the original rock on which the Virgin Mary stood.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by late Baroque architecture.
It is characterized by the Hodegetria style.
It is characterized by the Egyptian-Revival and Gothic styles.
It is characterized by the tallest steeple in Lithuania.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Salus Populi Romani icon.
It was influenced by the apparition.
IsA: It is a Roman Catholic image.
KnownAs: It is known as Our Lady of the Pine Woods.
It is known as Lithuania's greatest treasure.
It is known as Terra Mariana.
KnownFor: It is known for its veneration in Lithuania.
It is known for its ancient tradition of the Feast of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It is known for the apparition of the Virgin Mary.
It is known for its devotion to Mary.
LocatedIn: It is located at the Basilica of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Šiluva, Lithuania.
It is located in Washington, D.C.
It is located in Chicago, Illinois.
It is located in Putnam, Connecticut.
It is located in East St. Louis, Illinois.
It is located in Rincon, New Mexico.
It is located in Elizabeth, New Jersey.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Basilica of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Šiluva, Lithuania.
It occurred at the site where the church had stood.
It occurred at the Basilica's Memorial Hall.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during World War II.
It occurred during the summer of 1608.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
PartOf: It is part of the Basilica of the National Shrine of the Immaculate Conception.
It is part of the parish church of Saints Peter and Paul.
UsedAs: It is used as a pilgrimage site.
It is used as a chapel.
UsedFor: It is used for the Feast of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It is used for devotions to Mary.
It is used for the establishment of the Šiluva Chapel.
It is used for promulgating knowledge of and devotion to Our Lady.
UsedIn: It is used in the Basilica of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It is used in the Basilica of the National Shrine of the Immaculate Conception.
It is used in a chapel in Washington, D.C.
It is used in shrines in Chicago, Illinois.
It is used in shrines in Putnam, Connecticut.
It is used in shrines in East St. Louis, Illinois.
It is used in a small mission church in Rincon, New Mexico.
It is used in the parish church of Saints Peter and Paul in Elizabeth, New Jersey.
UsedTo: It is used to invoke for those who have lapsed from the faith.
It is used to invoke for those who pray for them.

それはイリノイ州イーストセントルイスの聖堂で使われています。
それはニューメキシコ州リンコンの小さな宣教教会で使われています。
それはニュージャージー州エリザベスの聖ペテロとパウロ教区教会で使われています。
UsedTo: それは信仰から離れた人々のために、また彼らのために祈る人々のために呼びかけられるために使われます。
それは彼らのために祈る人々のために呼びかけられるために使われます。
